mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, boosting near-term corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term momentum trading and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and restrictive monetary policy creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and increases discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and rising consumer income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or near zero across all rolling 2-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth crosses below 1.0% quarter-over-quarter, it indicates severe demand contraction and recessionary conditions, which drastically lower earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 70% of quarters where GDP growth drops below the 1.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate healthy inventory turnover and strong downstream demand, triggering short-term positive sentiment and S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns during supply chain disruptions, or the interaction term loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.
